title: Roundup Tough Weedkiller, Ready to Use, Spray, 1 Litre
features: Kills tough weeds to the roots
Kills brambles, thistles, nettles and other deep rooted and woody weeds
Fast action, results visible in 1-2 days
Includes special guidance for treatment of tough and woody seeds
Children and pets need not be excluded from treated areas (once dry)
description: From the manufacturer Previous page Kills tough and woody weeds Kills most perennial, tough, deep rooted, woody weeds around the garden such as nettles and bracken. Kills right to the roots Travels through the leaves straight to the root to prevent weeds from growing back or regenerating. Results in 48 hours Our fast acting formula means visible results in just 48 hours, working right in front of your eyes! Degrades naturally in soil to allow replanting Degrades naturally in soil leaving no residue, meaning you can start replanting in just 7 days. Next page 1 Kills Tough/Woody Weeds 2 Kills To The Root 3 Results In 48 Hours 4 Replant In 7 Days Tough and effective weed control that gets to work within hours of application Kills tough weed to the roots. Works on those brambles, thistles, nettles, dock, couch and many more. The glyphosate formula is naturally broken down in 7-8 days by micro-organisms in the soil so you can get planting again. Our Pump 'N Go sprayer system allows up to 3 minutes of continuous spraying, with an extendable wand to get you closer to the weeds without bending, so you can remove weeds faster and easier. Why should I use ROUNDUP Tough Ready To Use Weedkiller? ROUNDUP Tough Ready To Use Weedkiller is absorbed by leaves and moves through the entire plant, above and below ground, to destroy nettles, ground elder, brambles, bracken and other tough and woody weeds. Where can I use ROUNDUP Tough Ready To Use Weedkiller? Controls most perennial, tough and deep rooted and woody weeds around the garden. Ideal for controlling unwanted vegetation and clearing areas for planting. Do not use on lawns or other wanted plants, unless you want to kill them. When should I use ROUNDUP Tough Ready To Use Weedkiller? Spray when weeds are actively growing and have adequate leaf area to absorb spray. Treat established perennial weeds at the start of flowering to give best results. Spray nettles and ground elder when new leaves have unfolded fully in late spring. How do I use ROUNDUP Tough Ready To Use Weedkiller? Apply spray evenly to wet the leaves of the weeds, avoiding run-off. Leave weeds 7 days before digging or soil cultivation, for the weedkiller to move to the roots. Repeat application may be necessary, should new growth occur. Is ROUNDUP Tough Ready To Use Weedkiller safe to use on areas used by children and pets? Keep children and pets away from treated areas until spray has dried. Keep out of the reach of children.
